macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. futures point lower as investors watch oil and geopolitics
items.summary: U.S. stock futures were lower in premarket trade, with S&P 500 futures down 0.25%, Dow futures down 0.53%, Nasdaq futures down 0.29%, and Russell 2000 futures down 0.45%. Crude oil also fell 1.7% to 70.52, suggesting energy markets were a key driver for risk sentiment.

items.title: Week ahead: heavy U.S. economic data and Fed stress-test results
items.summary: Markets are set to focus on a dense run of U.S. data, including S&P Global PMIs, Richmond Fed manufacturing, housing data, GDP, personal income and spending, jobless claims, trade balance, and Michigan consumer sentiment. Fed bank stress-test results are also scheduled, alongside earnings from Micron, FedEx, Darden, Paychex, Carnival, KB Home, McCormick, Acuity Brands, and BlackBerry.

items.title: Wall Street and the economy remain focused on inflation, spending, and gas prices
items.summary: Recent coverage highlights falling U.S. gas prices below $4 for the first time since March, along with retail sales showing consumers are still spending. The backdrop has kept inflation, growth, and energy prices at the center of market attention.

items.title: Markets remain resilient despite geopolitical and macro headwinds
items.summary: Fortune reports that the S&P 500 has risen nearly 80% over the past five years and the Nasdaq more than 86% despite pandemic, war, inflation, and Middle East conflict shocks. The article cites AI investment, steady unemployment around 4.3%, and GDP growth around 2% as ongoing supports for market optimism.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Agrees to Build Chips with Intel Amid US Manufacturing Push
items.summary: President Trump confirmed that Apple has agreed to manufacture chips with Intel, marking a major shift in semiconductor production as the US seeks to bring chip manufacturing back from Taiwan and other countries.

items.title: Epic Games Announces Major Launcher Redesign with Player Profiles and Universal Controller Support
items.summary: Epic Games revealed a significant redesign of its launcher, introducing player profiles with avatars, support for universal controllers, and numerous new features for gamers.

items.title: Numerous Older Apple Devices Hit with Unpatchable Bug
items.summary: Researchers identified an unpatchable vulnerability in older Apple devices due to immutable code, recommending migration to newer hardware as the most effective mitigation.

items.title: GTA 6 Preorders Begin Next Week with Official Cover Art Confirmed
items.summary: Rockstar Games confirmed that preorders for GTA 6 will launch next week, accompanied by the official cover art, though the final price remains unannounced.

items.title: The Oura Ring 5 Launches at $399 with Strong Appeal for First-Time Buyers
items.summary: The new Oura Ring 5 fitness tracker is now available at $399, offering compelling features for first-time buyers while providing less upgrade incentive for existing older model owners.

items.title: Xbox Plans Significant Layoffs as New CEO Overhauls Microsoft Gaming Division
items.summary: Microsoft's Xbox division is preparing for major layoffs as its new CEO initiates a strategic overhaul, with studios in the division facing potential closures.

items.title: Nothing Axes Upcoming Budget CMF Phone Due to RAM Shortage
items.summary: Nothing has canceled its planned budget CMF phone due to global RAM shortages, disrupting its product lineup and affecting consumer expectations.

items.title: Amazon MGM Studios Drops Upcoming OpenAI Movie
items.summary: Amazon MGM Studios decided to release an upcoming movie featuring OpenAI through a different studio, stating it would be better served elsewhere.
